codeReview.summary: The documentation packet at docs/modules/core-audit-runtime-settings.md is thorough, accurate, and well-structured. All source file paths referenced exist at their claimed locations. Key claims about @Async+REQUIRES_NEW on AuditService, HMAC signing in EnterpriseAuditTrailService, synchronous @Transactional in AccountingEventStore, and the facade pattern of TenantRuntimeRequestAdmissionService are verified against source code. The packet correctly distinguishes three audit surfaces (platform audit, enterprise audit trail, accounting event store) with their write semantics, failure modes, and de-dup contract. The runtime-gating split across three layers is clearly documented with coupling notes. The global-versus-tenant settings risk section provides a frank assessment of shared-table key-based isolation risks. The duplicate INDEX.md row was correctly removed. Cross-links to core-security-error.md, core-idempotency.md, ADR-004, AUDIT_TRAIL_OWNERSHIP.md, and other referenced documents are all valid. The scope note in core-security-error.md was correctly updated to link to the new packet. MODULE-INVENTORY.md received the appropriate cross-cutting infrastructure entry. No blocking issues found.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker discovered that the previous worker (1e31222b) left a duplicate row in INDEX.md for core-audit-runtime-settings. The orchestrator note in the feature description already flagged this, but the root cause — a previous worker adding a new row without checking for an existing one — suggests a gap in the documentation-ia.md library guidance. Adding a note about deduplication checks when adding rows to tabular docs would help future workers avoid this.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature description contains: 'partial drafts already exist... finish and validate that draft state, including removing the duplicate index row'. Worker transcript shows Edit tool removing the duplicate. docs/modules/INDEX.md line 33 now has single entry.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The architecture.md library file does not currently document the dual runtime enforcement service pattern (TenantRuntimeEnforcementService vs TenantRuntimeAccessService), which was a significant area the worker had to investigate from source code. This pattern — two independent enforcement services with independent caches reading from the same system_settings table — is architecturally important and could benefit from being captured in the architecture library.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker transcript shows reading TenantRuntimeEnforcementService.java, TenantRuntimeAccessService.java, TenantRuntimeEnforcementInterceptor.java, and TenantRuntimeRequestAdmissionService.java to understand the split. .factory/library/architecture.md does not mention the dual-enforcement pattern.

summary: PASS. The core audit/runtime/settings documentation packet is comprehensive, accurate, and well-structured. All three required areas are covered: (1) audit-surface ownership model clearly distinguishes platform audit, enterprise audit trail, and accounting event store with verified source code claims; (2) runtime-gating split documents three enforcement layers with coupling notes; (3) global-versus-tenant settings risk provides honest risk analysis. The duplicate INDEX.md row was properly cleaned up. All cross-references resolve to existing documents. Source file paths are accurate. The lint-knowledgebase.sh validator passed. No blocking issues. Two non-blocking shared-state observations noted about documentation-ia guidance and architecture library coverage.
